questions.question: Which of the following best describes this situation?
questions.scenario: An organization is reviewing a security situation involving a security control carried out by people and day-to-day processes.
questions.explanation: The key clue is a security control carried out by people and day-to-day processes. Operational control is the security term for that idea: a security control carried out by people and day-to-day processes. On the exam, choose Operational control when the scenario describes this specific behavior, control, attack, or process rather than a broader or unrelated security concept.
questions.lesson: 1.1 Compare and contrast various types of security controls.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes this situation?
questions.scenario: An organization is reviewing a security situation involving a failure mode that permits access when a control fails.
questions.explanation: The key clue is that the control failure still permits access. Fail-open favors availability: if the control cannot make a decision or stops working, traffic or users are allowed through. Contrast this with fail-closed, which blocks access when the control fails.
questions.lesson: 1.2 Summarize fundamental security concepts.

questions.question: Change management is not just for implementing software updates or hardware changes. For example, version control refers to capturing changes made to important documents a company needs. What are some documents or data that would utilize version control? (Select the three best options.)
questions.explanation: Capturing implemented changes in code is important as it will allow for a quick reversion to a known good state of the code if a change causes problems. Capturing changes within diagrams ensures only the most recent diagrams will be available while archiving the previous documents, but still having them available for reference. Important data will vary from company to company, but tracking changes will allow references for only the most recent data and avoid confusion. Faxes do not fall under change management as they are one-time communications and do not need tracking or logging.
questions.lesson: 1.3 Explain the importance of change management processes and the impact to security.

questions.question: Which control most directly reduces SQL injection and cross-site scripting risk at the application boundary. The response must align with Security+ exam logic and operational best practices.
questions.scenario: A security practitioner is evaluating a workplace security situation.
questions.explanation: The key clue is reducing SQL injection and cross-site scripting at the application boundary. Input validation checks submitted data before processing it, rejecting unexpected characters, formats, or values that could become executable input.
questions.lesson: 2.2 Explain common threat vectors and attack surfaces.

questions.scenario: A threat actor sends data to a system in a way that changes the commands being sent to the observer and causes unauthorized processing.
questions.question: What type of attack is occurring?
questions.explanation: Injection attacks exploit insecure request or query processing by altering submitted data so the application performs unintended commands or returns unauthorized data.
questions.lesson: 2.2 Explain common threat vectors and attack surfaces.

questions.question: An IT admin receives an alert regarding an employee's web activity in which requested addresses are not written in plaintext, but contain entries such as %2e%2e%2f%2e%2e%2f%2e%2e%2f. What could this employee be attempting to do? (Select the two best options.)
questions.explanation: A directory traversal attack occurs when a website user requests a file in an attempt to get to the parent directory. If settings are not correct on the web server, it could allow a malicious actor enhanced privileges to the network. A canonicalization attack attempts to circumvent certain web server command filtrations by using the canonicalization of plaintext. If it is successful, it may allow code injection or directory traversal. A command injection attack attempts to cause the server to run operating system (OS) shell commands and return the output to the browser. A server-side attack causes the server to do some processing or run a script or query in a way that is not authorized by the application design.
questions.lesson: 2.2 Explain common threat vectors and attack surfaces.

questions.question: A company prepares to add additional protection to its networks due to ongoing attacks. It is adding resiliency and high availability services, such as load balancing and cluster services. What attacks could the company be experiencing?
questions.explanation: Denial of service (DoS) attack protections include high availability services such as load balancing and cluster services. Companies without these protections in place would have a hard time dealing with constant DoS attacks. An on-path attack occurs when a malicious user has found a way to intercept data transmitted before it arrives to the intended recipient. The capabilities added to the system would not protect against this attack. These types of protections would not protect against a rogue Access Point (AP), as a rogue AP attempts to gather information or perform DNS redirection. A cryptographic attack typically targets authentication systems and requires a system to use outdated or weak cryptography. The capabilities added to the system would not protect against this attack.
questions.lesson: 2.2 Explain common threat vectors and attack surfaces.

questions.question: While reading an IT-related website, a tech notices an anonymous user's post about a vulnerability in a popular freeware. This vulnerability allows a remote user to gain access to the system via the freeware and match the level of permissions the app has. What type of attack is the user's post alluding to?
questions.explanation: An application attack specifically targets a weakness within an Operating System (OS) or an installed application. The severity of the vulnerability will determine what a malicious actor can accomplish. A command injection attack attempts to cause the server to run OS shell commands and return the output to the browser. However, it is not related to application vulnerabilities. An injection attack exploits some unsecure way in which the application processes requests and queries. A forgery attack attempts to hijack an ongoing authenticated session to perform some action without the user's consent.
questions.lesson: 2.2 Explain common threat vectors and attack surfaces.

questions.question: An incident response team member identifies an attack on the system network. Upon further analyses, it is determined that a threat actor was attempting to send data to the system in a manner that would change the commands being sent to the observer. What type of attack is occurring in this situation?
questions.explanation: An injection attack exploits some unsecure way the application processes requests and queries. For example, a threat actor queries for information but alters the request to return more users' data than authorized. A replay attack works by capturing or guessing the token value and then submitting it to reestablish the session illegitimately. A forgery attack attempts to hijack an ongoing authenticated session to perform some action without the user's consent. Cross-site Scripting (XSS) can compromise session cookies, which could then lead to the prediction of future session cookies, compromising all future sessions.
questions.lesson: 2.2 Explain common threat vectors and attack surfaces.

questions.question: While on patrol, a security guard discovers signs of forced entry on a window leading into the HVAC control room. Upon closer examination, the security guard notices hardly any lights or sounds of machinery working in the room. These are indicators of what type of attack?
questions.explanation: A brute force physical attack is an attack against a physical aspect of a facility containing the physical hardware. Forcing a window and damaging HVAC equipment are excellent indicators of a brute force attack. An on-path attack is where a malicious attacker can place themselves between two recipients to intercept messages sent via the network. A distributed denial of service (DDoS) attack consumes a system's resources, so legitimate requests cannot go through. A network-based attack comes over the network and does not require being physically present at the hardware's location to interfere with it.
questions.lesson: 2.2 Explain common threat vectors and attack surfaces.

questions.question: An IT intern looks for information on previous network attacks, specifically indicators of attempted and successful replay, forgery, and injection attacks. Where could the intern find this information? (Select the two best options.)
questions.explanation: Universal Resource Locators (URLs) can encode some action or data to submit to the server host. Analysis of these URLs can provide indicators of session hijacking/replay, forgery, and injection attacks. An administrator typically configures web servers to log Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP) traffic that encounters an error or traffic that matches some predefined rule set. Web servers can preserve indicators of attempted and successful replay, forgery, and injection attacks. Buffer overflow is an attack attempting to append data outside the buffer. It would not maintain this type of information. Session cookies maintain information about a user or authenticated session. They would not maintain this type of information.
questions.lesson: 2.2 Explain common threat vectors and attack surfaces.

questions.question: Which attack category does this indicator belong to?

questions.scenario: Thousands of SYN packets are detected hitting a public-facing server in a short period of time.

questions.question: What is the MOST likely concern?

questions.scenario: During a desk inspection, a small unknown device is found between a keyboard cable and the workstation.

questions.question: Which mapping is correct?
options: Malware = endpoint, Physical = hardware, Network = traffic, Application = software input
Malware = traffic, Physical = software input, Network = hardware, Application = endpoint
Malware = hardware, Physical = endpoint, Network = software input, Application = traffic
Malware = software input, Physical = traffic, Network = endpoint, Application = hardware
questions.answer: Malware = endpoint, Physical = hardware, Network = traffic, Application = software input
questions.explanation: For fast Security+ matching, malware usually affects endpoints, physical attacks involve hardware or access, network attacks involve traffic, and application attacks abuse software input.
questions.scenario: A student is building a quick memory aid for matching indicators of malicious activity to Security+ attack categories.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.scenario: An intern reviewing logs notices that for several weeks, several MBs of data have been sent out at the same time every day.
questions.question: What could this indicate?
options: Teleworker activity and denial of service
Boot sector virus and logic bomb
questions.explanation: Regular outbound transfers can indicate collected reconnaissance data or unauthorized data exfiltration leaving the network on a schedule.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.scenario: A personal computer locks after receiving a file. The screen shows a countdown clock and threatens permanent file encryption unless money is paid.
questions.question: Which pair BEST describes this infection?
questions.explanation: Ransomware demands payment under threat. Crypto-ransomware specifically encrypts files or threatens loss of the decryption key unless payment is made.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: These are indicators of what type of attack?
questions.explanation: Forced entry and damage to physical equipment indicate a brute force physical attack against a facility or hardware location.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.scenario: Customers report very slow file access and more users are having trouble logging in. Systems show resource consumption and resource inaccessibility.
questions.question: These are typically indicators of what type of attack?
questions.explanation: DoS attacks consume resources and can make services inaccessible to legitimate users, causing slow connections and failed access attempts.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.scenario: Several computers show increased memory usage and repeatedly send network requests to random IP ranges. No one observed user intervention when the activity began.
questions.question: What type of malicious activity is MOST likely happening?
questions.explanation: Computer worms self-replicate and spread across networks without user intervention. Repeated requests to random IP ranges and increased memory usage fit worm behavior.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.scenario: A security team is assessing a weakness, exposure, or remediation path and must choose the best vulnerability management concept.
questions.question: A company has recently deployed a new mobile application for its employees. During a security audit, observations show some employees downloaded the application from third- party app stores, not the official ones. Additionally, the IT department found that a few employees are using older versions of the operating system on their devices. What vulnerabilities are likely to emerge in this scenario?
options: Operating system-level vulnerability + Social engineering vulnerability
Operating system-level vulnerability + Outdated hardware vulnerability
questions.explanation: Downloading applications from third-party app stores increases the risk of installing versions that someone may have tampered with or may contain malicious code, introducing application-level vulnerabilities. Using older versions of an operating system could expose the devices to vulnerabilities patched in later versions, leading to operating system-level vulnerabilities. The scenario does not mention any outdated hardware. The issue is with the operating system versions and the source of the application download. The scenario does not describe a situation in which someone manipulates or deceives users into divulging sensitive information, which characterizes social engineering vulnerabilities.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: An IT security analyst at a mid-sized company has observed unusual network activity on a workstation over the past few days. This workstation has initiated frequent and unsolicited communications with an unknown external IP address. Further investigation reveals the presence of unauthorized software on the workstation, which seems to be actively transmitting sensitive system data to this external address and possibly receiving commands or files in return without any visible signs or knowledge of the user. Given these specific behaviors, what type of malware is MOST likely responsible for these activities?
questions.explanation: Once installed, an attacker can use a RAT to manipulate the system and exfiltrate data, which corresponds to the excessive communication with an external IP address and unauthorized software in the scenario. Potentially Unwanted Programs (PUPs) typically cause annoyances like displaying unwanted advertisements, altering browser settings, or slowing down the system, rather than stealing data or communicating with an external IP. A virus, a type of malware, can attach itself to a program or file, enabling it to propagate from one computer to another, leaving infections in its wake. While rootkits can enable malicious activities, they typically do not engage in the direct communication with an external IP that the scenario describes.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: The network administrator of a company receives an email notification about an unusual email activity. Multiple employees received an email with an attached file having an odd double extension: .docx and .ps1. The email system's security feature flagged the email as potentially harmful. Based on the provided details, what type of virus is MOST likely involved in this scenario?
questions.explanation: A script virus uses the programming features available in local scripting engines for the OS and/or browser, such as JavaScript. The scenario mentions an attached file with a .jar extension and is executable. While a memory resident virus could infect a file attached to an email, the scenario suggests a different type of virus. A boot sector virus infects the boot sector of a hard disk or a floppy disk. The suspicious file came through an email, which does not suggest a boot sector virus. Non-resident viruses or file infectors typically latch onto executable files and programs. Although the attachment is executable (.jar), the main indicator is the file's double extension, which suggests a script virus.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: An IT intern prepares and connects used laptops to the company network. The intern discovers that some of the laptops have degraded performance and decreased storage. What is commonly associated with potentially unwanted programs? (Select the two best options.)
options: It spreads through concealment within the executable code of another process.
It is software that is installed alongside a package selected by the user.
It is malware concealed within an installer package that appears legitimate.
It is also known as grayware or bloatware.
answer: It is software that is installed alongside a package selected by the user.
It is also known as grayware or bloatware.
questions.explanation: Potentially unwanted programs (PUPs) include software that is installed alongside a package selected by the user or bundled with a new computer system. Potentially unwanted programs (PUPs) are sometimes described as grayware rather than malware and can also be referred to as bloatware. A trojan is a form of malware that is concealed within an installer package for software that appears to be legitimate. Viruses and worms represent some of the first types of malware and spread without any authorization from the user by being concealed within the executable code of another process.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: The cybersecurity team of a company notices suspicious activities on its network. Some computers have increased memory usage and are sending out network requests repeatedly to random IP ranges. No one observed an intervention when these activities started. Based on the provided details, what type of malicious activity is MOST likely happening in this scenario?
questions.explanation: Computer worms are self-replicating malware that can spread across networks without user intervention. The continuous network requests to random IP ranges and increased memory usage indicate a worm's behavior. Viruses usually require user intervention, like opening an infected file or attachment. They also typically write themselves to the disk, which does not align with the observed behavior in the scenario. Trojans often disguise themselves as legitimate software and rely on user intervention for execution. The description in the scenario does not indicate user involvement, making this option unlikely. While ransomware can cause an increase in memory usage, it is typically associated with file encryption, and the user usually receives a ransom note, neither of which takes place in the scenario. Copyright © CompTIA, Inc. All rights reserved.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: Companies that have existed for a long time typically employ many different types of servers and software. A lack of vendor support coupled with high complexity, poor documentation, and business criticality raises significant security problems in the "seasoned" hardware and software. What are these types of hardware and software commonly referred to as? (Select the two best options.)
questions.explanation: Legacy applications pose unique challenges for change management as these systems are critical to business operations yet built using outdated technology that cannot accept updates. Legacy systems pose their own unique problems when issues arise while attempting to install the latest software or patches. If the patch is uninstallable, the system is a security vulnerability waiting for exploitation. Fit-gap software refers to created in-house software that allows the legacy systems to function with updated software or legacy applications to function on new hardware. Emulation involves virtual machines creating a legacy machine/system capable of running legacy software on newer hardware.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: An earthquake occurred near the company HQ, causing severe damage in the area. The earthquake affected the building, which will not be usable for several weeks. What plan will the company follow to maintain its business? (Select the two best options.)
questions.explanation: The continuity of operations plan (COOP) focuses on the critical processes that must remain operational during and after a substantial disruption, like a natural disaster. A disaster recovery plan details the steps required to recover from a catastrophic event such as a natural disaster. The goal is to restore operations as quickly and efficiently as possible. The incident response policy outlines the processes to follow after a security breach or cyberattack occurs. An acceptable use policy (AUP) outlines how individuals may use network and computer systems by defining what constitutes acceptable behavior by users.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: Due to the introduction of security vulnerabilities during a previous change, company leadership wants reassurance the vulnerabilities will not happen again. The IT department has made several changes to its change management plan. What are areas the IT department would add to this plan? (Select the three best options.)
questions.explanation: Impact analysis identifies and assesses the potential implications of a proposed change, including how the change will impact all areas. Evaluation of changes in a test environment ensures they work as intended. Test results provide valuable insight into the likelihood of success without impacting business operations. A backout plan is for reversing changes and returning systems and software to their original state if the implementation plan fails. A well-defined backout plan helps to minimize downtime and reduces the risk of data loss or other severe impacts. Stakeholders are personnel with a vested interest in completing the change, but they are not components of a change management plan that would keep vulnerabilities from appearing.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: A company has been experiencing issues with operator fatigue within the cybersecurity team, leading to decreased alertness and cognitive function. Considering different strategies to help combat this issue, how can automation and orchestration assist in addressing operator fatigue in security operations?
options: By enforcing standardized baselines and overriding unauthorized changes
By automating routine tasks, allowing cybersecurity personnel to focus on more complex, strategic issues
By increasing the complexity of the company's systems and processes
By reducing the initial cost of implementing security measures
questions.answer: By automating routine tasks, allowing cybersecurity personnel to focus on more complex, strategic issues
questions.explanation: By automating routine tasks such as scanning for vulnerabilities, applying patches, or monitoring systems, automation and orchestration can significantly reduce a cybersecurity team's workload. This reduces operator fatigue by allowing cybersecurity personnel to focus on more complex, strategic issues. While automation and orchestration might save costs in the long run due to increased efficiency and reduced errors, the initial cost of implementation can be high. While this is a benefit of automation and orchestration, it does not directly address operator fatigue. This benefit maintains consistent system configurations and security. Increased complexity is actually a potential challenge of implementing automation and orchestration, not a benefit, and does not address operator fatigue.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: A global logistics company implements a complex automation and orchestration system to enhance security infrastructure. Concerned about potential misconfigurations and vulnerabilities that may arise from integrating various tools and platforms, the security team evaluates different methodologies to ensure a robust and secure deployment. Which of the following strategies would be MOST appropriate for minimizing the risk of misconfigurations and vulnerabilities in the automation and orchestration system?
options: Implementing automation without understanding underlying security policies and controls
Outsourcing the automation development to the lowest bidder without considering security expertise
Relying solely on vendor-supplied security features without additional validation or testing
Utilizing a threat modeling approach to identify potential vulnerabilities during the design phase
questions.answer: Utilizing a threat modeling approach to identify potential vulnerabilities during the design phase
questions.explanation: Utilizing a threat modeling approach identifies and mitigates potential vulnerabilities during the design phase, providing a proactive strategy to minimize risks in the automation and orchestration system. Implementing automation without understanding underlying policies may lead to misconfigurations not aligning with the company's goals for a robust and secure deployment. Relying solely on vendor-supplied features without validation may not provide comprehensive security and not align with the company's objectives for minimizing risks. Outsourcing to the lowest bidder without security consideration may introduce vulnerabilities, failing to meet the company's requirements for secure automation.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: A healthcare provider's IT manager wants to automate routine tasks within its network security management. This automation will free up IT staff time and enhance system defenses. Potential risks are a concern, and the manager wants to implement automation securely. What strategies should the IT manager prioritize to achieve secure automation, considering both efficiency and potential risks?
options: Implementing a manual approval process for all automated tasks
Introducing a proprietary system for automation without collaboration with security experts
Utilizing automation tools with built-in security features and compliance monitoring
Outsourcing the development of automation scripts to a specialized cybersecurity firm
questions.answer: Utilizing automation tools with built-in security features and compliance monitoring
questions.explanation: Utilizing automation tools with built-in security and compliance monitoring efficiently provides risk mitigation, aligning perfectly with the IT manager's overarching goals for secure automation for the healthcare provider. Implementing a manual approval process would significantly slow down automation, reducing efficiency and not aligning with the IT manager's goals for streamlined security management and automation for the healthcare provider. Introducing a proprietary system without expert collaboration may lead to a lack of security expertise, risking the IT manager's secure automation objectives and overall security stance for the healthcare provider. Outsourcing may introduce external dependencies and lack the required customization, potentially falling short of the IT manager's specific security and automation needs and expectations for the healthcare provider.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: In an IT environment, automation and scripting play a critical role in managing services and access. How does automation assist security analysts in their daily tasks?
options: By improving the efficiency of ticketing platforms
By enabling and disabling services, modifying access rights, and maintaining the lifecycle of IT resources
By facilitating the development of more complex systems such as SOAR platforms
By helping with user and resource provisioning
questions.answer: By enabling and disabling services, modifying access rights, and maintaining the lifecycle of IT resources
questions.explanation: Automation and scripting are essential tools for managing services and access within an IT environment. This includes enabling or disabling services, modifying access rights, and maintaining the lifecycle of IT resources, which directly aligns with the tasks of security analysts. While automation assists in user and resource provisioning, the question specifically asks how automation assists security analysts in managing services and access, not provisioning. While improving the efficiency of ticketing platforms is a benefit of automation, it does not directly apply to the tasks of security analysts in managing services and access within an IT environment. While automation does facilitate the development of more complex systems like Security Orchestration Automated Response (SOAR) platforms, this is not a direct way that it assists security analysts in managing services and access.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: A multinational corporation is upgrading its IT infrastructure to enhance security governance and streamline its change management process. The IT department is considering various strategies to accomplish this update. Which strategy MOST effectively achieves the corporation's goals, considering the inherent risks and benefits?
options: Implementing automation and scripting to perform tasks quickly and efficiently
Manual monitoring of security controls and change management protocols
Using proprietary security solutions without automation
Outsourcing the entire IT operations to a third-party vendor
questions.answer: Implementing automation and scripting to perform tasks quickly and efficiently
questions.explanation: Automation and scripting enhance efficiency and security, which aligns with the corporation's goals. The IT department should use automation and scripting with care to prevent potential risks. Automation improves monitoring efficiency by making it faster, better, and cheaper than manual monitoring. Manual monitoring does not help the corporation achieve its goal of making its processes more efficient. Instead, it slows down the process, lowers the quality, and increases the cost. Outsourcing may fail to directly address the corporation's security governance and change management needs. It may also lead to complexities and loss of control. Proprietary solutions without automation lack efficiency. They do not align with the corporation's objectives for streamlined processes.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: A manager reprimands an IT employee because the employee did not follow instructions on the server build. Each server's configuration was different, including different software and settings. What should the employee have followed to build the server correctly?
questions.explanation: A standard defines the expected outcome of a task, such as a particular configuration state for a server or performance baseline for a service. Following the standard for each build would ensure each server's configuration would match. Access control models define appropriate access models for different use cases. It does not deal with the configuration of servers. Organizational policies are vital in establishing effective governance and ensuring organizational compliance. They form the framework for operations, decision-making, and behaviors, not for the configuration of servers. Guidelines describe recommendations that steer actions in a particular job role or department. However, they are not strict standards to follow to ensure security.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: Given the importance of automation and orchestration related to secure operations, a newly hired IT employee creates, modifies, and deletes user accounts and access rights across the company's IT systems. Due to the significant number of users and the heightened need for security, this task proves to be time-consuming and error-prone. Which automation capability can not only improve the efficiency and consistency of this task but also ensure secure operations?
questions.explanation: Creating, modifying, or deleting user accounts and access rights across IT systems is termed as provisioning. This ensures secure operations by maintaining consistency and adhering to security protocols. While ticketing is an essential part of IT operations, it primarily deals with managing support requests and incidents, not user and access management. Service management involves tasks like enabling or disabling services and maintaining IT resources, but it does not specifically involve creating, modifying, and deleting user accounts and access rights. While guardrails and security groups play a vital role in managing security within an organization, they do not directly involve creating, modifying, or deleting user accounts and access rights. Instead, they provide a framework for managing security and defining resource access.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: An organization must hire nonaffiliated experts (consultants) to conduct an assessment. The organization expects the experts to provide a broad evaluation of their overall performance, practices, and capabilities, including specific focus areas, such as strategy, operational efficiency, risk management, cybersecurity, or compliance practices. Which type of assessment meets the organization's needs?
questions.explanation: External experts or consultants conduct an external assessment to evaluate an organization's overall performance, practices, capabilities, or specific focus areas. Individuals or organizations conduct self-assessments internally to evaluate their performance, practices, and adherence to established criteria against predetermined metrics and measures without the involvement of external experts. An organization conducts an internal compliance assessment to ensure that its operating practices align with laws, regulations, standards, policies, and ethical requirements without involving external experts. The internal audit committee provides independent oversight and assurance regarding an organization's financial reporting, internal controls, and risk management practices without involving external experts.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: A risk manager at a large corporation conducts a Risk and Control Self-Assessment (RCSA) to identify and assess risks for a new market expansion. The manager identifies risks associated with new regulatory requirements, market volatility, and aggressive competitors. Which Key Risk Indicator (KRI) metric is MOST critical for managing these risks?
options: The frequency of security incidents involving sensitive customer data
The number of regulatory violations reported by regulatory authorities
The level of market volatility currently affecting the corporation
The number of new customers acquired in the new market expansion
questions.answer: The number of regulatory violations reported by regulatory authorities
questions.explanation: The most critical metric is the number of reported regulatory violations. The risk manager must prioritize ensuring regulatory compliance in a new market. Non-compliance can result in fines, damage to reputation, and operational disruption. While important for business growth, customer acquisition does not directly measure risk exposure. The company cannot directly control market volatility, an external factor. Instead, the company should develop strategies to manage the effects of volatility. Security incidents pose significant risks, but regulatory compliance is the more immediate concern during expansion. The risk manager should manage security risks, but in this context, the focus should be on regulatory risks.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: An organization must ensure its operating practices align with laws, regulations, standards, policies, and ethical requirements. The organization wants to evaluate the effectiveness of internal controls, identify any noncompliance or risk areas, and communicate findings to internal stakeholders such as risk managers. Which internal assessment approach would be MOST appropriate for this purpose?
questions.explanation: A compliance assessment ensures that operating practices align with laws, regulations, standards, policies, and ethical requirements through an internal evaluation. This approach is the most appropriate for the scenario described. While an audit committee may participate in the compliance process, it does not specifically evaluate compliance with laws and regulations. A self-assessment, which can serve various purposes, including compliance, does not specifically evaluate compliance with laws and regulations. External regulatory authorities or agencies typically perform a regulatory assessment to ensure compliance with specific laws, regulations, or industry standards, not as an internal assessment approach.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: A large healthcare organization is considering an alliance with a third-party medical software provider. The organization wants to ensure they well-document all aspects of the alliance, with both parties understanding their roles and responsibilities. Which of the following should the healthcare organization prioritize to clearly define the parameters, the expectations for both parties, and the protocols for managing risks and security?
options: Service level agreement (SLA) and statement of work (SOW)
Business Partnership Agreement (BPA) and Rules of Engagement (RoE)
Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) and nondisclosure agreement (NDA)
questions.answer: Business Partnership Agreement (BPA) and Rules of Engagement (RoE)
questions.explanation: A BPA sets the terms for long-term strategic partnerships, including objectives, financial arrangements, decision-making processes, intellectual property rights, and dispute-resolution mechanisms. The RoE establishes the boundaries and expectations of the partnership, covering roles, security requirements, compliance obligations, reporting and communication protocols, change management procedures, and contractual provisions related to risk management. Parties usually use an MOU and NDA as initial steps before entering a more formal agreement. An SLA and SOW, though important, typically apply to specific projects and service provisions rather than forming the overall framework of a business partnership. Questionnaires gather information about a vendor's security practices and risk management strategies, while an MSA sets out the general terms of a particular contract.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: A large financial institution is considering outsourcing its IT infrastructure to a third-party cloud service provider. The company has concerns about the risks of giving its sensitive financial data to an external vendor. What approach should the company use to ensure the vendor complies with the appropriate security standards and regulations?
options: Enter into a contract without clauses for regular assessments or audits of the vendor's security practices.
Prioritize the vendor's cost and ease of use over security considerations based on their capabilities.
Ensure the vendor consents to and undergoes regular penetration testing to verify their security practices.
Rely on the vendor's reputation in the industry without the need to conduct any further assessments.
questions.answer: Ensure the vendor consents to and undergoes regular penetration testing to verify their security practices.
questions.explanation: Penetration testing identifies potential vulnerabilities in a vendor's systems, networks, and applications, assessing their security posture. The company gains insights into the vendor's security resilience and vulnerabilities that attackers could exploit by conducting or requesting evidence of regular penetration tests. Simply entering a contract without provisions for regular assessments or audits may be insufficient. Regular assessments and audits ensure ongoing adherence to security standards and compliance requirements. Solely relying on the vendor's reputation may not offer a comprehensive and objective assessment of the vendor's current security practices and regulatory compliance. While important, cost and ease of use should not take precedence over security considerations.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: A risk manager for a company providing IT support services conducts a business impact analysis (BIA) and identifies a Mission Essential Function (MEF) that relies on a server with a Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) of 2,500 hours and a Mean Time to Repair (MTTR) of 4 hours. Given a Maximum Tolerable Downtime (MTD) of 24 hours and a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 6 hours for this function, what should the risk manager prioritize in the risk management strategy?
options: Increasing the MTD for the function
Extending the RTO for the function
Reducing the MTTR of the server
Improving the MTBF of the server
questions.answer: Reducing the MTTR of the server
questions.explanation: Reducing the MTTR is crucial. With an RTO of 6 hours and an MTTR of 4 hours, other activities have limited time, such as recovery during the maximum tolerable downtime. Lowering the MTTR provides more time for those activities. The current MTBF of 2,500 hours is substantial and not an immediate concern considering the MTD of 24 hours. Increasing the MTD might not be feasible or desirable. The risk manager sets the MTD based on the disruption the business can tolerate. Extending the RTO is not the best option. The current RTO of 6 hours seems adequate, considering the MTTR of 4 hours. The focus should be on restoring the server within the RTO.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: The IT security team of a company has concerns about network vulnerabilities and hires an external penetration tester to evaluate its security controls and identify potential risks. The company provides the penetration tester with fragments of network information and permits them to use reconnaissance techniques for further information gathering. What penetration testing method is the company using?
questions.explanation: Partially known environment penetration testing involves the tester's limited knowledge about the target system or network. During the test, the tester may employ reconnaissance techniques to gather additional information about the target. Known environment penetration testing involves the tester having detailed knowledge about the target system or network, including information about the network architecture, hardware and software configurations, system vulnerabilities, and users. Unknown environment penetration testing involves the tester having little knowledge about the target system or network. This testing mimics a scenario where an attacker has no preexisting information about the target infrastructure. Open-source intelligence gathering is a passive reconnaissance technique that collects publicly available information from various sources. It is not a penetration testing method. questions.question: A growing company's IT department is weighing the pros and cons of different architectural models for its next project. The debate narrows down to cloud architecture versus traditional network architecture. During a team meeting, the head of IT security asks a newly hired network specialist to identify the primary security consideration when comparing cloud architecture to traditional network architecture. Based on the conversation in the IT department, which security consideration is MOST directly associated with cloud architecture compared to traditional network architecture?
options: Requirement for secure physical access to network devices
Encryption of data transmitted over local networks
Shared responsibility model with service providers
Need for regular network hardware/firmware updates
questions.answer: Shared responsibility model with service providers
questions.explanation: In cloud services, the division of security responsibilities between the cloud service provider and the customer creates a shared responsibility model. Regularly updating firmware is crucial for defending against emerging threats. This practice is necessary for cloud and traditional network architectures to maintain a strong security posture. The phrase "local networks" emphasizes data transmission within the confines of a particular physical location or company infrastructure. However, it aligns more with traditional network architecture than cloud architecture. For traditional network architectures, a heightened emphasis exists on ensuring controlled physical access to network devices, preventing unauthorized interventions. In contrast, cloud architectures often leverage the robust infrastructure of service providers, trusting them to handle the intricacies of physical hardware management and security.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: A multinational corporation is preparing to launch a new cloud service that will store customer data from various countries. The chief privacy officer (CPO) must ensure the service complies with cross-border data transfer regulations and individual rights under different national and international laws. What is the CPO considering?
options: Auditing physical access controls at data centers to ensure compliance
Ensuring adherence to global data transfer frameworks and agreements
Updating the corporation's firewall configurations for the new cloud service
Implementing region-specific data minimization practices for data collection
questions.answer: Ensuring adherence to global data transfer frameworks and agreements
questions.explanation: Adherence to international frameworks is crucial in a multinational environment where customer data is moving across borders. This adherence to international frameworks reflects the global scale at which the CPO needs to ensure compliance. Auditing physical access controls at data centers is impractical to ensure compliance with cross-border data transfer regulations and individual rights under various national laws. Implementing region-specific data minimization practices ensures that the company only collects and retains the minimum necessary data for each region. However, it is secondary to the broader concern of cross-border data transfer compliance. Updating firewall configurations for the new cloud service is an important aspect of securing the service, but it depends on compliance with privacy regulations across various countries.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: As part of a business continuity plan, a company wants to create a resilient work model that securely allows employees to access critical network resources, regardless of physical location. This model must enforce strict access controls and multifactor authentication while facilitating on-site and remote work capabilities. What type of work environment mostly aligns with these requirements?
questions.explanation: A hybrid work environment is a flexible work model that allows employees to work both on-site and remotely. It aligns with the company's need for a resilient work model to maintain business operations under various conditions. While a fully remote work environment does allow for secure access controls and multifactor authentication, it does not facilitate on-site work capabilities. On-premises work environments restrict employees to working full-time from the company's physical office location. This environment does not meet the company's goal of allowing for secure remote work capabilities. A third-party outsourced work environment does not align with the scenario, which specifies a resilient work model for existing employees and does not necessarily satisfy the stringent security requirements outlined.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: Which of the following accurately reflects the responsibilities of a data processor under data protection laws such as the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)?
options: The data processor determines the purposes and means of processing personal data.
The data processor has independent decision-making power over personal data.
The data processor processes personal data on behalf of the data controller.
The data processor is directly responsible for obtaining consent from data subjects.
questions.answer: The data processor processes personal data on behalf of the data controller.
questions.explanation: The data processor processes personal data on behalf of the data controller and acts under the authority and instructions of the data controller. The data processor is not allowed to make decisions alone regarding the processing of the data. The responsibility of determining the purposes and means of processing personal data lies with the data controller, not the data processor. The data processor does not have independent decision-making power over personal data. The data processor can only process data when the data controller instructs the data processor to do so. The responsibility of obtaining consent from data subjects lies with the data controller, not the data processor.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: The company’s security team notices a significant increase in security incidents stemming from employee errors and decides to focus on proactive strategies to reduce these incidents. What approach educates through gamification and allows employees to engage in simulated security scenarios? (Select the best two options.)
questions.explanation: Implementing user guidance and training is a direct approach to educating employees about the security risks associated with their actions and the proper protocols to follow, thereby reducing the likelihood of security incidents due to employee errors. Tabletop exercises allow employees to engage in simulated security scenarios, which can provide hands- on experience and education. Bolstering security policies could indirectly support educating employees by defining the expected standards and behaviors. However, it is not the best choice without additional context suggesting that policies are currently weak or absent. Handbooks could serve as a practical reference for employees, but they might not be as engaging or as immediate a solution as active training sessions.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: In an organization’s security operations center (SOC), analysts continuously identify anomalies, scrutinize network traffic patterns, and generate regular summaries of security incidents to inform senior management. Which practice is central to effectively maintaining this constant awareness of the security status and promptly communicating the essential details to the relevant stakeholders?
questions.explanation: Reporting and monitoring is a fundamental practice in an SOC. Monitoring involves continuously observing network and system activities to detect irregular patterns that may signify a security incident and regularly summarizing and communicating these findings to relevant stakeholders. Incident response planning focuses more on the preparedness and handling of an actual incident rather than observation of network activities and regular communication of security statuses to stakeholders. Log analysis and review identifies anomalies, but it does not encompass the broader aspect of reporting these findings to relevant stakeholders. Threat intelligence gathering does not, in itself, represent the ongoing process of observing network and system activities and summarizing these findings for regular communication to senior management.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.question: A company uses a popular password manager. It noticed unusual breaches in its systems and forced a password reset on all employees' accounts. What is a primary consideration when using third-party software for any computer function?
options: Every vendor is at risk of threats.
The company cannot hire employees with specific roles.
Costs can be cheaper than doing it all internally.
questions.answer: Every vendor is at risk of threats.
questions.explanation: There are risks when using any software, including third-party vendor services or software. It is important to analyze a vendor’s security posture to protect against breaches. Costs are typically cheaper using a third party for a task or software. However, in the case of password managers, free alternatives eliminate this as an option. Simply using a third-party vendor does not necessarily lower the risk. In some instances, it increases the risk to a company even more than developing something internally. Not being able to hire talent for a specific role can limit what a company can accomplish internally and may force its hand to export a task or project externally.
questions.lesson: 2.4 Given a scenario, analyze indicators of malicious activity.

questions.scenario: A security analyst is documenting a control or condition involving a two-door physical access control designed to prevent tailgating.
questions.explanation: The key clue is a two-door physical access control designed to prevent tailgating. Access control vestibule is the security term for that idea: a two-door physical access control designed to prevent tailgating. On the exam, choose Access control vestibule when the scenario describes this specific behavior, control, attack, or process rather than a broader or unrelated security concept.

On the exam, choose Record encryption when the scenario describes this specific behavior, control, attack, or process rather than a broader or unrelated security concept.

Sideloading is the security term for that idea: installing an application from outside an approved application store. questions.scenario: A security analyst is documenting a control or condition involving a cloud model where the provider manages servers and customers deploy functions or services.
questions.explanation: The key clue is a cloud model where the provider manages servers and customers deploy functions or services. Serverless computing is the security term for that idea: a cloud model where the provider manages servers and customers deploy functions or services. On the exam, choose Serverless computing when the scenario describes this specific behavior, control, attack, or process rather than a broader or unrelated security concept.
questions.lesson: 3.1 Compare and contrast security implications of different architecture models.

questions.scenario: A cloud architecture team is reviewing segmentation or cloud design choices and must choose the best architecture principle.
questions.question: A network engineer reviews the security implications tied to cloud architecture models as the company plans to move data off-premises at the end of the year. What model provides flexibility by allowing the company to store sensitive data to a private cloud infrastructure and non-sensitive information on a public cloud infrastructure?
questions.explanation: In this scenario, a hybrid architecture provides greater flexibility and control over sensitive data and applications, allowing customers to store data on private cloud infrastructure while using public cloud infrastructure for less sensitive workloads. A multi-tenant architecture is a cost-effective model by allowing multiple customers to share the same infrastructure, with each customer's data and applications being separated logically between the customers. A serverless architecture involves a cloud provider managing the infrastructure and automatically scaling resources up or down based on demand. A single-tenet architecture provides dedicated infrastructure to a single customer, ensuring that only the customer can access the customer. This model offers the highest level of security as the customer has complete control of the infrastructure.
questions.lesson: 3.1 Compare and contrast security implications of different architecture models.

questions.scenario: A cloud architecture team is reviewing segmentation or cloud design choices and must choose the best architecture principle.
questions.question: The Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) for a regional textile company tasks a cyber team with reviewing the security implications on available cloud architecture models as the business anticipates transitioning to a cloud environment within the next year. What model offers the highest level of security and allows the individual customer to have complete control over the infrastructure?
questions.explanation: In this scenario, a single-tenant architecture provides dedicated infrastructure to a single customer, ensuring that only the customer can access the customer. This model offers the highest level of security as the customer has complete control of the infrastructure. A multi-tenant architecture is a cost-effective model by allowing multiple customers to share the same infrastructure, with each customer's data and applications being separated logically between the customers. A hybrid architecture provides greater flexibility and control over sensitive data and applications, allowing customers to store data on private cloud infrastructure while using public cloud infrastructure for less sensitive workloads. A serverless architecture involves a cloud provider managing the infrastructure and automatically scaling resources up or down based on demand.
questions.lesson: 3.1 Compare and contrast security implications of different architecture models.

questions.question: Which pair is commonly associated with potentially unwanted programs?

Concealment in executable code and self-replication
Legitimate-looking installer package and hidden malware payload
Boot sector infection and partition table modification

questions.explanation: PUPs are often bundled with selected software or new systems and are commonly described as grayware or bloatware rather than clearly malicious software.
